## Summary

Adds \`MemorySessionIngestor\` — a deterministic service that lifts Memory Core artifacts (session summaries + raw per-turn memories) into the Native Edge Graph as first-class \`SESSION\` and \`MEMORY\` nodes with \`ORIGINATES_IN(Memory → Session)\` edges. Wires it into the REM cycle so every processed session populates the graph structure *before* \`SemanticGraphExtractor\` runs.

Resolves #10151. Phase 1 of the Graph-first Memory artifacts sub-epic (#10143).

## Why

Prior to this PR, memories and summaries existed only as Chroma rows — extracted entities (concepts, classes, methods) became graph nodes but their source memories did not. Downstream work streams depend on this asymmetry being closed:

- **Mailbox (#10139)** needs \`Message → IN_REPLY_TO → Memory\` edges — memories must be nodes first.
- **AgentIdentity ownership (#10016)** needs \`Memory → AUTHORED_BY → AgentIdentity\` edges — same prerequisite.
- **Gemma4 provenance edges (#10152)** needs \`Concept → MENTIONED_IN → Memory\` — extractor can attach to existing nodes instead of dangling at \`sessionId\` scalars.
- **Lazy back-fill (#10153)** needs the schema + ingestion logic this PR establishes.

## What's in This PR

- \`ai/daemons/services/MemorySessionIngestor.mjs\` (new, 173 lines) — singleton service mirroring \`ConceptIngestor\` pattern. Upserts SESSION + MEMORY nodes keyed by Chroma IDs. Differential sync via SHA-256 \`payloadHash\` on node properties.
- \`ai/daemons/DreamService.mjs\` — adds import + invocation in \`processUndigestedSessions\` session loop, *before* \`executeTriVectorExtraction\`. One new timing log line matching the Tri-Vector / Topology / TEST_GAP phase log pattern.
- \`test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/services/MemorySessionIngestor.spec.mjs\` (new, 6 tests) — TDD coverage of stats shape, happy path, idempotency via hash-match skip, zero-memory edge case, metadata-change re-upsert, input validation.

## Design Rationale (Prescription Refinements Against Ticket Body)

Ticket-intake surfaced two prescription refinements versus the ticket body's literal wording. Both align with established codebase patterns:

### 1. Service class, not method on DreamService

The ticket body proposed a method on \`DreamService\`. But the codebase pattern (\`ConceptIngestor\`, \`IssueIngestor\`, \`FileSystemIngestor\`, \`GraphMaintenanceService\`) delegates ingestion work to dedicated singleton services at \`ai/daemons/services/\`. DreamService orchestrates; services do the work. Creating \`MemorySessionIngestor.mjs\` matches precedent and keeps DreamService lean.

### 2. No separate schema migration file

Ticket AC #1 called for a \`better-sqlite3\` migration adding Memory + Session node types. But \`GraphService.upsertNode\` treats \`type\` as a free-form label string — \`ConceptIngestor\` added \`CONCEPT\` nodes without any migration. Node labels aren't schema-constrained. A migration would be overbuild. Indices on JSON properties *would* require migration, but linear scans on 794 sessions / 8246 memories are fine at current scale; index optimization is a follow-up candidate if perf surfaces.

### 3. \`memoryCollection\` parameter as a test seam

\`syncSessionToGraph(session, {memoryCollection = null} = {})\` accepts an optional collection. Production callers pass nothing → falls back to \`StorageRouter.getMemoryCollection()\`. Tests inject stubs with seeded data. No module-level monkey-patching, no global-state mutation, no test-only branches in production code.

## Testing

### New spec: 6/6 pass (636ms)

\`\`\`
test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/services/MemorySessionIngestor.spec.mjs
- should return stats object with the documented shape
- should upsert one SESSION node plus N MEMORY nodes plus N ORIGINATES_IN edges
- should produce identical graph state across two consecutive sync calls (idempotency)
- should upsert only the SESSION node when the session has no memories
- should re-upsert a memory when its metadata changes (payload hash mismatch)
- should reject input missing session.meta.sessionId and return an error
\`\`\`

Serial mode (\`test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})\`) applied — the cold import cascade of Neo core + GraphService + SystemLifecycleService under Playwright's default fullyParallel mode triggered ESM partial-evaluation races (\`ClassSystem.mjs does not provide default\`). Serial runs imports once; warm module cache is reused across tests. Comment in the spec documents the reasoning.

### Baseline regression check

Ran \`DreamService.spec.mjs\` both with and without the DreamService edit (via \`git stash\` isolation). Baseline has 4 pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR — all concept-graph gap inference (\`GUIDE_GAP\` / \`EXAMPLE_GAP\` / \`ORPHAN_CONCEPT\` / combined), asserting \`capabilityGap\` properties on concept nodes. **Same 4 fail with my edit stashed out** → not a regression caused by this PR. Worth flagging as a separate diagnostic ticket but out of scope here.

### \`DreamServiceGoldenPath.spec.mjs\` — all pass.

## Edge Cases & Open Questions

- **Memory metadata shape assumption.** Ingestor hashes \`{sessionId, createdAt, userId}\` for differential sync. If Memory Core adds more metadata fields in the future, they won't contribute to the hash — unchanged metadata will skip upsert even if downstream fields drift. Follow-up ticket if richer metadata surfaces; acceptable for v1.
- **\`userId\` propagation.** Ingestor writes \`userId\` from metadata when present, but doesn't enforce — #10016 Multi-Tenant Identity work will layer enforcement. v1 is permissive; OAuth2 (#10145) tightens.
- **Node labels not in \`GraphService.getOrphanedNodes\` protection list.** The function protects \`SYSTEM_ANCHOR\`, \`System\`, \`ISSUE\`, \`DISCUSSION\`, \`PULL_REQUEST\` from orphan cleanup. \`SESSION\` and \`MEMORY\` are currently unprotected. If graph maintenance GC runs before mailbox or identity edges land, nodes could be pruned as orphans. Not blocking for v1 (no GC cycle currently runs against these labels) but worth tracking as follow-up.
- **Concurrency races during lazy back-fill** (#10153 scope) — tracked as Avoided Trap in #10143 per Gemini's epic-review; mitigation (\`INSERT OR IGNORE\` or mutex) lives in #10153 implementation.

## Test Plan

- [ ] Gemini reviews this PR (cross-model, enhanced pr-review skill with Depth Floor + Cross-Skill Integration Audit)
- [ ] Merge after approval
- [ ] Next REM cycle observes \`[DreamService] -> Memory/Session graph ingestion took: Xs (N upserted, M skipped)\` log output
- [ ] Post-shipping: graph integrity check — every MEMORY node has exactly one \`ORIGINATES_IN\` edge; no orphan MEMORY nodes without session parents
- [ ] Unblocks #10152 (Gemma4 provenance edges), #10153 (lazy back-fill), #10158 (telemetry + retention)

> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics

> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The `memoryCollection` dependency injection seam parameter is an excellent architectural takeaway for enabling unit testability over asynchronous internal singleton systems without global monkey-patching.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge:**
> The PR body highlights the fact that `SESSION` and `MEMORY` labels are not in `GraphService.getOrphanedNodes`' protection list. The author assumed this is "Not blocking for v1." I actively challenge this assumption: leaving them unprotected creates a ticking time bomb. If graph maintenance GC is triggered manually or by an adjacent agent workflow before the mailbox or identity edges land, these newly created nodes will be silently wiped out, causing cascading failures in the downstream pipelines that rely on them. Adding these strings to the protection list in `GraphService` is a trivial, immediate fix that eliminates the risk entirely.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔗 Cross-Skill Integration Audit
> 
> *N/A - This PR does not introduce new workflow primitives, conventions, or modify existing `SKILL.md` documents.*
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 📋 Required Actions
> 
> To proceed with merging, please address the following:
> 
> - [ ] Add `SESSION` and `MEMORY` to the protected labels list inside `GraphService.getOrphanedNodes` to prevent premature garbage collection.
